blocks.text: Disassemble and reassemble front driveshaft

items: Parts to be disassembled shall be marked to ensure that they are assembled in the same position when assembled.

blocks.text: 1. Boots Bunt removal
Remove the boot band.

items: Be careful not to damage the boots.

items.text: Use the band pinch (A) (commercially available tool) to pinch and remove the band (B).
Use the band pinch (A) (commercially available tool) to pinch and remove the band (B).
For the locking clip band (C), raise the folded part (D) and raise the lever part (E) to remove it.
For the locking clip band (C), raise the folded part (D) and raise the lever part (E) to remove it.

blocks.text: 2. Inboard joint removal
Wipe off grease from inboard joint (A). Align inboard joint and roller (B) and mark (C). Wrap with a rag (D) and remove the inboard joint.
3. Remove roller and spider
Alignment marks (C) are placed on the roller (A) and spider (B). Remove the roller from the spider. Align mark (E) on spider and drive shaft (D).
Align mark (B) with drive shaft (A).
Spider (A) is supported by vise (B). Remove the drive shaft from the spider using the special tool.

items: Do not pinch the drive shaft (C) with the vise.
Do not tap the drive shaft directly with a hammer.

blocks.text: Remove the stop ring (B) from the drive shaft (A).

Remove the circlip (A). Alignment marks (D) are placed on the roller (B) and spider (C). Remove the roller from the spider. Alignment mark (F) on spider and drive shaft (E). Remove spider from drive shaft.

items: If the spider/driveshaft joint is tight, use a puller to remove it.

blocks.text: 4. Inboard boot removal
Wrap vinyl tape (C) from the end of the drive shaft (B) to the spline to prevent damage to the inboard boot (A). Remove the inboard boot from the drive shaft. Remove the vinyl tape.
1. Boot band removal
Pull up the tabs (A) on the joint side and the shaft side (3 tabs on each side) with a screwdriver, etc., and remove the boot band (B).

items: Be careful not to damage the boots.

blocks.text: 2. Outboard joint removal
Move the outboard boot (A) to the inboard joint side.

items: Be careful not to damage the boots.

blocks.text: Wipe off any grease in the driveshaft and outboard joints. Place a scale (B) on the end of the outboard joint (A) and align it with the drive shaft (C) and mark (D).

Wrap the drive shaft (A) with a rag (B) and press it down with a vise (C) so as not to damage it. Attach the special tool to the outboard joint (D). Pull the outboard joint from the drive shaft. Remove the drive shaft from the vise.
Remove the stop ring (B) from the drive shaft (A).
3. Outboard boot removal
Wrap vinyl tape (C) from the end of the drive shaft (B) to the splines to prevent damage to the outboard boot (A). Remove the outboard boot from the driveshaft. Remove the vinyl tape.
1. development

items: All disassembled parts other than rubber parts shall be cleaned with washing oil and washed with an air gun to remove the oil.

blocks.text: 1. Inboard boot mounting
Wrap vinyl tape (C) from the end of the drive shaft (B) to the spline to prevent damage to the inboard boot (A). Install the inboard boot onto the driveshaft. Remove the vinyl tape.
2. Roller, Spider Mounting
Check the size of the new stop ring.

items: Be sure to check the size of the stop ring, as installing a stop ring other than the specified one will damage the driveshaft and the vehicle.

blocks.text: Install a new stop ring (B) in the groove (A) on the drive shaft.
Connect the drive shaft (B) to the spider (C) while pressing the stop ring (A).
Holding the drive shaft vertically, drop the drive shaft by its own weight from a height of about 10 cm above the floor, and insert the drive shaft into the spider.

items: Place a rag on the floor and take care not to damage the spider.
Do not use hammers.

blocks.text: To avoid damaging the drive shaft (A), place it between pieces of wood (B) and press it with a vise (C). Using the special tool, install the stop ring into the ring groove in the spider.
Check that the alignment mark (A) on the drive shaft is aligned with the spider (B).

items: Ensure that the stop ring is securely in the spider mounting groove to prevent damage to the driveshaft or vehicle.

blocks.text: Attach the roller (A) to the spider (C), paying attention to the orientation of the roller (A) and aligning the alignment marks (B).
Align the alignment marks (A) and install the spider (B) on the drive shaft (C). Install the circlip (D) into the circlip groove (E) on the drive shaft.
Attach the roller (B) to the spider (C) by aligning the alignment marks (A).
3. Inboard joint installation
Lubricate inboard joints and inboard boots with grease.

rows.text: Vehicles not equipped with a turbo charger
Turbo Charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger

blocks.text: Install the inboard joint by aligning the inboard joint (A) with the alignment mark (B) on the roller.

items: Be careful not to drop the roller (C).
After the inboard joint is assembled, be careful not to point the inboard joint downward as it will fall.

blocks.text: Align the inboard boot (A) with the groove (D) in the inboard joint (B) and drive shaft (C) and cover it.

items: Care should be taken to prevent grease from adhering to the boot mounting groove.

blocks.text: 4. Between inboard and outboard joints Length adjustment
Adjust the space between the inboard and outboard joints to the specified dimensions.

items: The air pressure in the boot shall be atmospheric when the length between joints (A) is within the standard value to prevent deformation of the boot.

rows.text: rabbit horns (used as a metaphor for things that do not exist)

blocks.text: 5. Boot Band Installation
Wrap the new locking clip band (A) around the inboard boot (B) and bend the lever part (C). Fold the clip section (D).

items: Do not reuse the removed low-profile band.

blocks.text: Place the new low-profile band (A) into the inboard boot (B).
Attach the tab (B) to the specified position using the band pinch (A) (commercial tool).

items: The boot band should be tightened slowly until a clicking sound is heard.
Do not tighten after confirming the clicking sound, as it may cause grease leakage.

blocks.text: Check that the boot band is properly installed. Make sure the boot band is not loose and wipe off any grease or other contaminants on the boot or driveshaft surface.

items: If the boot band tip (A) is not installed properly, replace it with a new boot band and reassemble according to the procedure.

blocks.text: 1. Outboard Boot Mounting

items: An Omega band must be used on the outboard side of the boot.

blocks.text: Wrap vinyl tape (C) from the end of the drive shaft (B) to the splines to prevent damage to the outboard boot (A). Install two new Omega bands (D) and outboard boot to driveshaft. Remove the vinyl tape.

items: The Omega Band must be set in the mounting groove of the outboard boot in advance, as it will be deformed if it is installed after the outboard joint has been installed.

blocks.text: 2. Outboard joint installation
Check the size of the new stop ring.

items: Be sure to check the size of the stop ring, as installing a stop ring other than the specified one will damage the driveshaft and the vehicle.

rows.text: Vehicles not equipped with a turbo charger
Turbo Charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger

blocks.text: Install a new stop ring (B) in the groove (A) on the drive shaft.
Fill the outboard joints with 35 g of the specified grease.

rows.text: Vehicles not equipped with a turbo charger
Turbo Charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger

blocks.text: Insert the drive shaft (C) into the outboard joint (B) until the stop ring (A) is hit.
Holding the outboard joint and driveshaft vertically, drop them by their own weight from a height of about 10 cm above the floor and install the stop ring in the ring groove in the outboard joint.

items: Spread a rag on the floor and be careful not to damage the drive shaft end (threaded part).
Do not use hammers.

blocks.text: Place a scale (B) on the end of the outboard joint (A) and check that it is aligned with the alignment mark (C) on the drive shaft.

items: Ensure that the stop ring is securely in the mounting groove of the outboard joint to prevent damage to the driveshaft or vehicle.

blocks.text: Lubricate grease in outboard joints and outboard boots.

rows.text: Vehicles not equipped with a turbo charger
Turbo Charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger: Cars equipped with a turbo charger

blocks.text: Align the outboard boot (A) with the groove (D) in the outboard joint (B) and drive shaft (C) and cover it.

items: Care should be taken to prevent grease from adhering to the boot mounting groove.

blocks.text: 3. Between inboard and outboard joints Length adjustment
Adjust the space between the inboard and outboard joints to the specified dimensions.

items: The air pressure in the boot shall be atmospheric when the length between joints (A) is within the standard value to prevent deformation of the boot.

rows.text: rabbit horns (used as a metaphor for things that do not exist)

blocks.text: 4. Boot Band Installation
Using boot band pliers (A) (commercially available tool), tighten the omega points (B) of the two omega bands.
Check that the Omega point is of the specified dimension. Make sure the band is not loose and wipe off any grease or other contaminants on the boot or drive shaft surface.

items: If the Omega Point exceeds the specified dimensions, it shall be replaced with a new Omega band.
